The Los Alamos variable energy
cyclotron.
Two 300 Omega-cm gold-surface barrier
semiconductor detectors.
After traversing the detector-target
chamber, the beam was collected in a Faraday cup, and
the integrated beam was monitored with a current
integrator. Baffles were placed around the detectors
so that only particles from the target could be
detected.
BP
All of the targets except radium
were prepared by evaporation on nickel backings of
approximately 100-microg/cm2 areal density. 233U
targets had areal densities of 20-30 microg/cm2, and
it was 99% monoisotopic.
Fig. 3, page 2243.
All of the targets except radium were prepared by
evaporation on nickel backings of approximately
100-microg/cm2 areal density. The 209Bi targets had
areal density of 100-200 microg/cm2, which were
determined to an accuracy of 20% by measuring the
elastic scattering for 25.5-MeV 3He ions at 90 degree
and comparing with other measurements.
Fig. 4, page 2244.
The average final
total kinetic-energy release.
All of the targets except radium were prepared by
evaporation on nickel backings of approximately
100-microg/cm2 areal density. The 197Au target had
areal densities of 100-200 microg/cm2, which were
determined to an accuracy of 20% by measuring the
elastic scattering for 25.5-MeV 3He ions at 90
degrees and comparing with other measurements. The
target was monoisotopic.
For a given set of data, the events were sorted to
give the energy and mass spectra in each of the two
channels, the total kinetic energy release spectrum,
and a probability matrix for the total kinetic energy
release versus the mass ratio of the two fragments.
The average value of the final total kinetic energy
release and a value for the variance were computed
for each mass ratio group.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
The variance of the average total kinetic-energy
distribution.

All of the targets except radium

target had areal densities of 100-200 microg/cm2,
which were determined to an accuracy of 20% by
measuring the elastic scattering for 25.5-MeV 3He
ions at 90 degrees and comparing with other
measurements. The 206Pb target had an isotopic
composition of 88% 206Pb, 9% 207Pb, and 3% 208Pb.
